text.title: Demo Hypothesis Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Demo Hypothesis Template application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each demo MUST have a named hypothesis (segment + job + price-sensitivity) filed BEFORE the call. No hypothesis = it's a sales pitch, not a discovery demo.
rule.rationale: Continuous-discovery research — anchors Demo Hypothesis Template rule `hypothesis-named-pre-demo`.

rule.statement: Each demo MUST declare exactly one success metric (observable prospect behaviour: 'asks about pricing within 15 min', 'requests another call within 48h'). Multi-metric demos collapse into vibes.
rule.rationale: Goal-Question-Metric — anchors Demo Hypothesis Template rule `single-success-metric`.

rule.statement: Each demo MUST declare a kill signal (response that falsifies the hypothesis). No kill signal = hypothesis is unfalsifiable.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability discipline — anchors Demo Hypothesis Template rule `kill-signal-required`.

rule.statement: Next demo for the same hypothesis MUST NOT be booked until the previous demo's artefact is filed + reviewed. Prevents stacking demos that all test the same wrong thing.
rule.rationale: Demo-discipline research — anchors Demo Hypothesis Template rule `follow-up-gate`.

rule.statement: The filled template IS the demo agenda. Demos without the artefact are skipped or rescheduled.
rule.rationale: Agenda-discipline practice — anchors Demo Hypothesis Template rule `artefact-as-agenda`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
